---
title: Platform
---

Site reliability metrics for the CanadaLogin platform, in the `sre` schema.
Currently incident-response metrics, with other reliability measures to follow.

The relevant tables are:

- [`sre.mttr_current`](../catalog/sre.qmd#mttr_current) - latest state of every incident, one row per incident
- [`sre.mttr_history`](../catalog/sre.qmd#mttr_history) - dated snapshots of every incident, preserving how each evolved

These tables come from a hand-maintained spreadsheet; see the catalog
[Data quality](../catalog/sre.qmd#data-quality) note for caveats. The one that
shapes most queries here: the incident timestamps (`detected_at`, `resolved_at`,
and so on) are stored as America/Toronto strings, not native dates, so a
reporting window filters on the `YYYY-MM-DD` prefix of `detected_at` rather than
a `from_date` column.

## Incident Response Metrics

{{< include sre/mean-time-to-resolution.qmd >}}

## Site Uptime Metrics

Uptime and response-time metrics for the six monitored CanadaLogin sites, in
the `sre` schema. Data comes from the
[`cds-snc/status-statut`](https://github.com/cds-snc/status-statut) GitHub
repository and is fetched automatically by the `uptime-daily-download` Lambda
at 4 AM ET each day. No manual steps are required.

The relevant tables are:

- [`sre.uptime_summary`](../catalog/sre.qmd#uptime_summary) — one row per site
  per day; rolling-window uptime percentages and average response times
- [`sre.uptime_timeseries`](../catalog/sre.qmd#uptime_timeseries) — one row per
  upptime bot commit per site; individual ping events including outage detections

Both tables are partitioned by `date=YYYY-MM-DD`. Because `date` is a `varchar`
(derived from the Hive partition path), cast it before doing date arithmetic:
`CAST(date AS DATE)` in SQL; `as.Date(date)` in R.

{{< include sre/site-uptime.qmd >}}

{{< include sre/site-response-time.qmd >}}

{{< include sre/outage-events.qmd >}}
